snippets.title: Basic configuration and a module logger
snippets.code:
import logging

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s",
)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

log.debug("hidden by default")     # below INFO threshold
log.info("service starting")
log.warning("rate limit close")
snippets.explanation: basicConfig sets the root level and format once; getLogger(__name__) names the logger after the module.

snippets.title: Lazy formatting and exception logging
snippets.code:
import logging
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

model, tokens = "gpt-4o", 1280
log.info("call model=%s tokens=%s", model, tokens)   # args formatted only if emitted

try:
    1 / 0
except ZeroDivisionError:
    log.exception("model post-processing failed")    # logs message + traceback at ERROR
snippets.explanation: %-args avoid building strings for suppressed logs; log.exception attaches the traceback inside an except block.

snippets.title: Per-environment level and quieting a library
snippets.code:
import logging, os

level = os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
logging.basicConfig(level=getattr(logging, level, logging.INFO))

logging.getLogger("httpx").setLevel(logging.WARNING)   # silence chatty SDK
snippets.explanation: Verbosity is controlled by env var; a noisy dependency is raised to WARNING independently.
